relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Timothy appears at the crossing of Judean trust and Greek lineage.
The siblings witness well of him.
Paul adapts Timothy for mission among local Judeans.
The Jerusalem discernment is carried city to city.
Assemblies strengthen and multiply.
Holy Spirit redirects the route away from Asia.
The Spirit of Jesus closes Bithynia.
They pass to Troas.
A Macedonian plea appears in the night: come help us.
The vision is discerned as divine call to good news.
The journey crosses the sea toward Macedonia.
Philippi becomes the mission’s Roman-colony doorway.
Prayer by the river opens conversation with gathered women.
Lydia’s heart is opened to attend to the word.
Her household is baptized, and hospitality becomes mission-home.
An enslaved girl’s exploited divination confronts the prayer path.
She speaks true words through an unfree spirit system.
In Jesus Messiah’s name, the spirit leaves her.
When exploitation loses profit, her masters seize the apostles.
The charge becomes civic disturbance and ethnic suspicion.
Roman identity is used to reject the customs proclaimed.
Crowd and magistrates join in violence.
Beaten, they are thrown into prison.
The inner prison and stocks become the place of praise.
At midnight, prayer and hymns fill the listening prison.
Earthquake opens doors and loosens bonds.
The jailer sees open doors and moves toward self-destruction.
Paul’s great cry stops harm: we are all here.
The jailer trembles before those he guarded.
His question becomes salvation/healing: what must I do?
Trust in the Lord Jesus opens saving/healing for the household.
The word is spoken to the whole house.
He washes wounds and is baptized in the night.
A table replaces the prison, and household joy rises.
Morning brings official release.
The jailer offers peaceful departure.
Paul refuses secret dismissal after public injustice against Roman citizens.
The magistrates fear the rights they violated.
They appeal and ask the apostles to leave.
Lydia’s house receives them again before they encourage the siblings and depart.
